entries.title: Mounty Yarns: In Their Own Words
entries.excerpt: In the streets of Mount Druitt, a group of young people are rewriting the story written about them. Not with policy papers or government reports — but with their own voices.

entries.title: Mount Isa Bush Camp — First One on Country
entries.excerpt: The Mount Isa bush camp was our first big event. We took 12 young fellas out on country for three days.

entries.title: Doomadgee Camp — Taking It Remote
entries.excerpt: The community welcomed us with open arms. The Elders were so happy to see someone bringing activities for the young people.

entries.title: Tuesday Gym Sessions — Building Consistency
entries.excerpt: Every Tuesday we're at the PCYC in Mount Isa. Consistency is the key — these kids have had so many people come and go in their lives.

entries.title: Spinifex Residential — Getting Through to the Hard Cases
entries.excerpt: When we first started going in there, the staff were sceptical. After about the fourth session, the kids started waiting for us at the gate.

entries.title: Why I Started BG Fit
entries.excerpt: I started BG Fit because I saw too many of our mob going through the justice system and not getting the support they needed.

entries.title: Creating Our Own History
entries.excerpt: We still tell the past, but we also going to tell the future now. Our most important thing is our kids and their kids being connected. To protect this place.

entries.title: Bringing Kids Back to Country
entries.excerpt: Last month we took eight young people out to Atnarpa for three days on Country. Some of them hadn't been out bush since they were little kids. You could see it in their faces when we got there — something shifted.

entries.title: Coming Home to Love's Creek
entries.excerpt: It's been a long process thinking about the ownership of this land, but it's just good to be coming home. I grew up between here and Ross River as a kid, and it's good to be back home.
